questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = t^2$, $y = 2t$. The tangent at $t = 3$ meets the curve again at a second point. Find the parameter value there.

steps.explanation: The tangent at parameter $p$ is $py = x + p^2$; at $p = 3$ this is $3y = x + 9$.
Any point on the curve has coordinates $(t^2, 2t)$; substituting finds where the tangent meets the curve.
Bringing all terms to one side gives a quadratic in $t$.
This is a perfect square with a repeated root at $t = 3$.
The repeated root means the line touches the curve only at $t = 3$ — it is a genuine tangent and does not cross the parabola again.
For this parabola every tangent meets the curve exactly once, so there is no second point.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The tangent touches only at $t = 3$ (repeated root); there is no second intersection.
